title: phemeranthus validulus
intro: Phemeranthus validulus, also known as Hawaiian prickly poppy or Maui dwarf poppy, is a perennial succulent plant native to the island of Maui in Hawaii. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, Phemeranthus validulus is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves, and is also added to skincare products for its antioxidant and anti-aging properties.
